message:
feat(lab): implement DPOR-guided schedule explorer with trace monoid deduplication

Add DporExplorer that replaces random seed-sweep with targeted schedule
exploration guided by dynamic partial-order reduction:

- Detect data races in execution traces via detect_races()
- Derive new schedules from backtrack points at each detected race
- Use TraceMonoid fingerprints to deduplicate equivalent schedules,
  pruning redundant exploration across Mazurkiewicz equivalence classes
- Track coverage metrics including total races, backtrack points
  generated, backtrack points pruned, and exploration efficiency ratio
- Maintain a BFS work queue of pending seeds with explored-set tracking
- Export DporExplorer and DporCoverageMetrics from lab module

This provides sound coverage (at least one representative per reachable
equivalence class) while avoiding the exponential blowup of exhaustive
interleaving enumeration.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
